summarization.a: The figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns (2θχ) of the MnO2, 10cLi-MnO2 and 50cLi-MnO2 samples.
summarization.b: The figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns (θ−2θ) of the MnO2, 10cLi-MnO2 and 50cLi-MnO2 samples.

data_extraction.a:
| Sample intensity (arb. units) | 2θχ (°2θ) |
| --- | --- |
| 50cLi-MnO2 | Peaks at 20, 37, 65 |
| 10cLi-MnO2 | Peaks at 37, 65, 72 |
| MnO2 | Peaks at 37, 40, 65, 72, 79 |
data_extraction.b:
| Intensity (arb. units) | θ-2θ (°2θ) |
| --- | --- |
| 50cLi-MnO2 | Peaks at 15, 20, 33, 36, 42, 44 |
| 10cLi-MnO2 | Peaks at 20, 33, 42, 44 |
| MnO2 | Peaks at 20, 33, 42 |

a.question: Which two phases are observed among these three samples?
a.answer: The reflections match with Li1−xMn2O4 and β-MnO2.

b.question: What does the reflection at 41.3° correspond to?
b.answer: The  (200) reflection of the tetragonal pyrolusite MnO2.

b.question: Why is the reflection at 20.3° "forbidden"?
b.answer: The reflection at 20.3° is indexed as (100), which  is forbidden for symmetry reasons in the tetragonal pyrolusite space group P42/mnm, but it may appear if the residual stress in the film deforms the structure to be slightly orthorhombic instead of tetragonal.

b.question: What is the impurity at 32.6°?
b.answer: This reflection is most likely caused by a lithium-rich Li2−zMnO2 (ICDD PDF 38-1282) impurity phase, which has (100) reflection at 32.5° and (012) reflection at 47.6°.
